channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: American Ben Ogden wins silver, breaking 50 year medal drought for U.S. men's cross-country skiing
item.description: Ben Ogden of Vermont skied powerfully, finishing just behind Johannes Hoesflot Klaebo of Norway. It was the first Olympic medal for a U.S. men's cross...

item.title: Lutnick, Wasserman and Others Caught Up in Epstein Files
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Casey Wasserman, the entertainment mogul, are among those facing blowback amid the release of files related to J...

item.title: How much power does the Fed chair really have?
item.description: On paper, the Fed chair is just one vote among many. In practice, the job carries far more influence. We analyze what gives the Fed chair power.

item.title: Immigration Judge Says Rumeysa Ozturk, a Tufts Student, Cannot Be Deported
item.description: The student, Rumeysa Ozturk, who is from Turkey, was detained by immigration agents last year after she co-wrote a pro-Palestinian opinion article for...

item.title: An ape, a tea party — and the ability to imagine
item.description: The ability to imagine — to play pretend — has long been thought to be unique to humans. A new study suggests one of our closest living relatives can ...

item.title: DHS faces funding deadline. And, courts fast-track Somali asylum seeker hearings
item.description: Congress has until Friday to reach a deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security. And, several asylum cases filed by Somali migrants in immigrati...

item.title: How the Israeli President’s Visit to Australia Created a ‘Tinder Box’
item.description: Isaac Herzog’s trip led to widespread rallies and tested the restrictions on protests that Australia installed after a deadly attack on a Jewish celeb...

item.title: Trump Administration Claims About Shootings by Federal Agents Unravel in Court
item.description: Before the killings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti, allegations against four others shot at by federal immigration agents failed to withstand scrutiny.

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: How the Israeli President’s Visit to Australia Created a ‘Tinder Box’
item.description: Isaac Herzog’s trip led to widespread rallies and tested the restrictions on protests that Australia installed after a deadly attack on a Jewish celeb...

item.title: Eating Kosher in the Heart of Syria: Lamb-Stuffed Zucchini but Hold the Yogurt
item.description: In the post-Assad era, more Jews are visiting a country that some fled decades ago. One hotel restaurant offers a corner where religious dietary requi...

item.title: How Italy’s Police and Army Compete to Enlist Italian Olympians
item.description: Most Italian athletes at the Winter Games subsidize their training by joining the police or the military, which vie to enlist the best talent.

item.title: Olympic Officials Ban Ukrainian’s Helmet Honoring War Dead
item.description: Vladyslav Heraskevych called it a tribute to athletes killed in the war with Russia. The International Olympic Committee said it defied a ban on polit...

item.title: Watch: A Conversation With Anna Wintour and Her U.S. Vogue Successor, Chloe Malle
item.description: In their first joint interview, Ms. Malle spoke about filling the shoes of Ms. Wintour, who urged people to “get over comparisons.”

item.title: Chappell Roan Leaves Casey Wasserman Agency Over Epstein Files
item.description: Casey Wasserman, who founded and leads the talent agency bearing his name, exchanged flirtatious emails with Ghislaine Maxwell in the early 2000s.
